channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US warns of growing North Korean military support for Russia's war
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The U.S. and its allies are “alarmed” by North Korea’s nuclear and missile threats as well as its increasing military support for Russia’s war in Ukraine, a senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy outlines his 'victory plan' to Ukraine's lawmakers, including a call to join NATO
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told lawmakers Wednesday that Ukraine’s Western partners are increasing pressure to negotiate with Russia, but he hinted such talks would be unfavorable to Kyiv as he unveiled what he called his “victory plan” for the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NATO shows no sign of letting Ukraine join soon and wants more details about its 'victory plan'
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO showed no sign Wednesday it would invite Ukraine to join anytime soon as allies sought more information from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy about his “victory plan” for ending the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China's leader makes rare visit to province facing Taiwan following major war exercises
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China’s President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to a province facing Taiwan following military exercises that mobilized China's navy, air force, missile force and land troops to simulate a blockade of the self-governing island democracy that Beijing claims as its own territory a
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea confirmed Thursday that its recently revised constitution defines South Korea as “a hostile state" for the first time, two days after it blew up front-line road and rail links that once connected the country with the South.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zeta drug lord continued to control cartel from inside a Mexican prison, says US indictment
item.description: A U.S. indictment unsealed in the District of Columbia claims that the leader of one of Mexico's most violent gangs continued to control an offshoot group, the Northeast Cartel, from inside a Mexican prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 27 in Lebanon, including in a town with a dark history of civilian deaths
item.description: QANA, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes pounded areas across Lebanon, killing at least 27 people over the past 24 hours, officials said Wednesday, including more than a dozen in a southern town where Israeli bombardments in previous conflicts are seared into local memory.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD ZAATARI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Italian Senate passes law making it illegal to seek surrogacy abroad
item.description: Italy’s Senate approved legislation Wednesday criminalizing the seeking of surrogacy in other countries by Italian citizens. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni advocated for the policy, which is seen as targeting same-sex couples.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: In the heartland of Mexico's Sinaloa cartel, the old ways have changed and violence rages
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Cellphone chats have become death sentences in the continuing, bloody factional war inside Mexico's Sinaloa drug cartel. Cartel gunmen stop youths on the street or in their cars and demand their phones.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pressure grows for countries to deliver on promised biodiversity targets at UN conference
item.description: Two years after reaching a historic biodiversity agreement, countries will gather next week to determine whether they are making progress on efforts to save Earth's plant and animal life.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen Cotton says Biden-Harris likely prolonged Gaza war, let aid go to terrorists: 'Betrayed' taxpayers
item.description: Amid Israel’s existential seven-front war to root out terrorists on its borders, Arkansas GOP Sen. Tom Cotton says U.S. humanitarian aid might have got into the hands of terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine defense minister confident can replenish troops but in need of weapons, equipment from allies
item.description: As the war in Ukraine drags on with no end in sight and mounting casualties, the country desperately needs international assistance and must quickly train and equip new soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woodward compares Putin to Hitler, says Russian leader 'trying to play' Trump
item.description: Veteran journalist and author Bob Woodward compared Russian President Vladimir Putin to Nazi leader Adolf Hitler, claiming Putin is “trying to play” former President Trump, putting the U.S. in dangerous spot if he returns to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Australia's prime minister is criticized for buying a waterfront home during a housing crisis
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has been criticized for buying a multimillion-dollar waterfront home during a national housing crisis with federal elections just months away.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SEE IT: Suspect discovered hiding in couch on police bodycam while serving arrest warrant
item.description: Bodycam footage from police in the UK show a game of hide-and-seek that ended poorly for one suspect being served an arrest warrant over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US 'concerned' about reports of North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russia in Ukraine
item.description: The U.S. says it is "concerned" about reports of North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russian troops in the war against Ukraine, calling it "a new level of desperation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: In a move that underlines North Korea's growing anger with South Korea, North Korea has blown up sections of unused rail routes and roads that once connected the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US threatens Israel with arms shipment suspensions over Gaza conditions: Reports
item.description: The Biden administration is telling Israeli officials to take immediate measures to address the humanitarian situation in the Gaza Strip or risk cutting off access to key U.S. military assistance.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hereditary nobles have sat in Britain's Parliament for centuries. Their time may be up
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Like his ancestors for centuries, the Earl of Devon serves in Parliament, helping to make the laws of the land. But not for much longer.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greek official accuses EU of policy failure on migration as war and climate change fuel displacement
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — A Greek official on Tuesday criticized the outgoing European Commission for failing to forge a common policy to deport migrants, and she warned that war and climate change were increasing global displacement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel reveals Hezbollah special forces terrorist 'bunker' located under home with weapons, motorcycles
item.description: Israel's military has uncovered a bunker it says Hezbollah's special forces unit was using to plan a cross-border attack on Israeli citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Campaigning begins for Japan's parliamentary election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Official campaigning for Japan's Oct. 27 parliamentary election began Tuesday with new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba seeking a mandate for his policies and for reforms after the governing party’s political funds scandal.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. to send troops and air defense system to Israel
item.description: The Pentagon announced the U.S. military will send a Terminal High Altitude Defense battery and about 100 U.S. troops to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation
